texto: Why does it scare me to think I might be ordinary? I remember when I started first grade and I could hardly pay attention for fear I wouldn't learn to read and write. I didn't want to be like everyone else. I didn't want to have to learn. I wanted to know everything already
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Margaret Sartor: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de enfrentar incertezas com ação. O trecho “Why does it scare me to think I might be ordinary? I remember when I started first grade and I co…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, fear, individuality pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de individuality e reading.
